english: customs, that have sprung from heterogeneous elements; of behaviour: inconsistent; unheard of, prodigious, °čo-p'rúl (cho 'phrul) magic feats Tar.

*ya-ma-la* (ya ma la), Ü: ya-ma-la-po (ya ma la po), Ts.: *ya-ma-len-te* (ya ma len te), Lex.: = snyiṅ-po-méd-pa (snying po med pa), mi-bdén-pa (mi bden pa) vain, unstable, fickle, not to be trusted or depended upon.

1. wonder, miracle, supernatural occurrence, adopted also as the term for the miracles of Scripture Chr. Prot.; ltas-sam yá-mtsan či byuṅ (ltas sam ya mtshan ci byung) Dzl. what signs and wonders have happened? yá-mtsan-du °gyúr-ba (ya mtshan du 'gyur ba) Dzl. to happen, to come to pass in a marvelous manner; yá-mtsan-ste (ya mtshan ste) Pth. being a wonderful man; kyód-la °di-tsam rig-pa-méd-pa ni yá-mtsan-čeo (khyod la 'di tsam rig pa med pa ni ya mtshan che'o) that you are so ignorant is very strange (wonderful); *yá-tsem-po* (ya tshem po) C. marvelous, miraculous; yá-tsam-can (ya tsam can) id. Schr. — 2. wonder, astonishment, amazement, rgyál-po yá-mtsan čén-po skyés-te (rgyal po ya mtshan chen po skyes te) Tar. the king greatly wondering; *yám-tsan tsór-če* (yam mtshan tshor ce), *čò-če* (co ce) W. to wonder; yá-mtsan-gyi gnas-so (ya mtshan gyi gnas so) Tar. it is a thing to be wondered at; dé-tsam yá-mtsan-rgyu med (de tsam ya mtshan rgyu med) Mil. that is not so very astonishing.
1. Cs.: differing, diverse, yá-ya-ba (ya ya ba) diversity; yá-ya-bor gyúr-ba (ya ya bor gyur ba) Sch.: a subject of dispute, contrariety of opinion. — 2. gyá-ya (gya ya).
corselet and helmet, mail, armour, gsér-gyi (gser gyi) of gold; also fig. B.
Judah, ya-hu-dá-pa (ya hu dA pa) jew Chr. Prot.
a small mattock, hoe, čàg-yag (lcags yag) iron hoe, šíṅ-yag (shing yag) wooden hoe Ts.

prov. also jág-po (jag po), seldom in B., but otherwise common in C. and W. good, in all its significations, both as to men and things, = bzáṅ-po (bzang po); *dei pí-la di yág-po* (de'i pi la 'di yag po) W. for that purpose this is good, fit, serviceable; *yág-po ǰhé-pa* (yag po jhe pa) C., *čó-če* (co ce) W., c. q. la, to caress, to flirt, also in an obscene sense; yág-po yag-po (yag po yag po) well, well!

1. (accented), again, once more: likewise, also, further, frq.; yaṅ yaṅ (yang yang) Mil., yaṅ daṅ yáṅ-du (yang dang yang du) Tar., yáṅ-nas yáṅ-du (yang nas yang du) Dzl. again and again; joined to adj. and adv. denoting a higher degree, still: yaṅ čuṅ (yang chung) Mil. still smaller, °di či-gaṅ-las yaṅ dgá-ba žig byuṅ ('di ci gang las yang dga' ba zhig byung) Mil. that was still more pleasing than any thing before; yáṅ sgos (yang sgos) Mil. still more in detail; *yáṅ-ñoṅ-žaṅ* (yang nyong zhang) W. the third day before yesterday. — 2. (unaccented, throwing the accent back on the preceding word), after the final letters g, d, b, s, gen. kyaṅ (kyang), after vowels often °aṅ ('ang), also, too, the Latin quoque, ṅa yaṅ (nga yang), bdag kyaṅ (bdag kyang) I too; bu čé-ba yaṅ (bu che ba yang) Dzl. my eldest boy too; bsód-nams daṅ yaṅ ldán-pa (bsod nams dang yang ldan pa) Dzl. having also merit; yaṅ — yaṅ — (yang — yang —), both — and —; °di yaṅ — de yaṅ ('di yang — de yang) both this and that, pyi-rol yaṅ naṅ yaṅ (phyi rol yang nang yang) both outside and inside; followed by a negative, neither — nor; yaṅ (yang) singly, with a negative: not even, kar-šá-pa-ni gčig kyaṅ mi sbyín-no (kar sha pa ni gcig kyang mi sbyin no) Dzl. I shall not even give a cowry for it; yaṅ (yang) with a comparative (as above) still, sṅár-bas kyaṅ lhág-par (sngar bas kyang lhag par) still more than formerly; as effect of a preceding cause, so then, kyeu de yaṅ tse °das-so (khye'u de yang tshe 'das so) Dzl. so then the boy died, bsád-pa yaṅ graṅs-méd-do (bsad pa yang grangs med do) Dzl. so then there were people killed without number; emphat., even, riṅ-por ma lón-par smra yaṅ šés-so (ring por ma lon par smra yang shes so) Dzl. within a short time he was even able to speak; sṅa-čád kyaṅ (snga chad kyang) Dzl. even before this; kar-šá-pa-ni °bum yaṅ (kar sha pa ni 'bum yang) even so much as a hundred thousand cowries (I would give); also joined to a verbal root: thams-čád °dus kyaṅ (thams cad 'dus kyang) even if all without exception be gathered; although, btsal kyaṅ ma rnyed (btsal kyang ma rnyed) although they were seeking, they did not find, or, they were seeking indeed, but did not find; this latter turn is frequently used, where we use but, yet, nevertheless etc.
1. sbst. postscript. Cs. — 2. adv. again, afresh, anew C.
